claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

To start, **how old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 19 years old, his eligibility depends on whether he's in approved education or training, as Child Benefit can continue until age 20 if certain conditions are met.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. Now I need to understand your living and care arrangements for Alex.

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live somewhere else (for example, at college accommodation, with another relative, or independently)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to confirm your UK residency status.

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just one more question to ensure I have all the information needed:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- Alex is 19 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since Alex is under 20 and continuing in approved education, you qualify for Child Benefit. The benefit can continue until he turns 20, as long as he remains in approved education or training.

**Please note:** If Alex's circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education, starts work for 24 hours or more per week, or starts claiming certain benefits in his own right), you should notify HMRC as this could affect your entitlement.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can apply for Child Benefit through the standard application process. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and is currently in approved education (college doing A-levels). According to the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for him. You live in the UK, and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, all eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 19-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age extension criteria as he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels at college). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK, is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.

Alex lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for him. You live in the UK, and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, all eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Alex meets the age extension criteria as he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels at college). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK, is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
